hard.next_step: 10% confidence Apr27 Constant engagement positioning AI/I2O...Objective to influence RFP Mar 25 Currently reviewing market to launch tender by end of Q3 (Nov). Ariba is too manual and not able to fix bug with new version (next Gen). AI maturity, full configuration, Supplier Portal are of strong interest. Demo request by mid-April. Good engagement

north_star_verdict.headline: Buyer has gone quiet since the 31 March email (Chee Koon Tan on medical leave after surgery); the market review that would influence NUS's end-2026 tender has not visibly progressed, and only Zycus rep outbound has run since.
north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2027-03-31, about 268 days out. Qualified since 25 March 2026 (about 103 days in stage), no forward slip recorded. Last genuine two-way buyer touch was the 31 March 2026 inbound email from Chee Koon Tan; every activity since (through 22 May) is Zycus rep outbound (Clari/Outreach emails, VIP event invites) with no buyer reply. Buyer's own timeline: open tender end of 2026 (Nov), one-year Ariba extension, selection into Q1 2027, which puts the realistic decision 4-6 months past a placeholder SF close. Required pace: restart the four expert evaluation sessions and re-confirm the tender window within 30 days to stay positioned pre-RFP.
evidence: Last genuine two-way buyer exchange was Chee Koon Tan's 31 March 2026 inbound email asking how suppliers who refuse to register are handled; no buyer reply on any thread since.
Every Salesforce activity from 24 April to 22 May 2026 is Zycus rep outbound (Clari/Outreach emails and Procurement AI World Tour / Horizon 2026 SEA invites) that went unanswered.
Chee Koon Tan has been recovering from surgery (rep emails 12 and 15 May reference his recovery), a documented reason for the silence rather than lost interest.
Economic buyer Chong Ning Pang (CPO), whom Chee Koon Tan named as 'my boss did ask me about Zycus', has never joined a call 103 days into Qualified.
SAP Ariba is the incumbent with internal IT air cover ('our IT department is very insistent that we give SAP a chance') per the 25 March 2026 call.
Buyer is running a broad four-way market review (SAP Ariba, iValue, GEP, Coupa) ahead of an open tender, per Chee Koon Tan on 25 March 2026.
Rep confidence logged at 10% on 27 April 2026; objective is to influence the RFP, not to close a live cycle.

metrics.narrative: Chee Koon Tan (Associate Director) articulated strong, specific operational pain on the 25 March 2026 call but never quantified it in dollars, hours, or headcount. He described SAP Ariba's inability to track spend at the framework-agreement level ('a framework agreement means I award a contract to 3 suppliers... it should be able to track at the framework level... if it crosses, it should flag out, and I don't have to manage individually'), a two-step supplier registration that forces NUS to 'outsource to a team to manage the registration process, and that's consuming money', and the burden of specification review and recommendation-report generation that 'sometimes can take months'. The closest to a metric is context, not a value case: NUS runs 'over a billion dollars more purchase annually' under headcount pressure ('we are asked to reduce some headcount, the work doesn't end'). No cost of the 79 pain points, cycle-time delta, or savings target has been captured, so the move is to quantify the manual-workaround cost with the CPO's team before the tender.
sources: Avoma Introduction Call 25 March 2026, Chee Koon Tan (framework agreement, supplier registration, spec/recommendation-report pain)
SF AIS_Why__c / Next_Step__c 25 March 2026 ('AI maturity, full configuration, Supplier Portal are of strong interest')

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is Chong Ning Pang, Chief Procurement Officer (Salesforce OpportunityContactRole and a task/activity contact). Chee Koon Tan effectively named him on the 25 March 2026 call as the sponsor behind this look at Zycus: 'my boss did ask me about Zycus'. Pang is therefore mapped by title and is the CPO who owns the procurement transformation and the tender budget, but he has never joined a call and has not replied to any of the seven direct outreach emails and Horizon/AI World Tour invites sent to him between 24 April and 22 May 2026. So the EB is identified but entirely passive: authority is clear, engagement is zero. The move is to convert Chee Koon Tan's relationship into a direct Pang introduction and put the AI/I2O influence-the-RFP narrative in front of him before the tender scope is written.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Chong Ning Pang, Chief Procurement Officer
Avoma 25 March 2026, Chee Koon Tan: 'my boss did ask me about Zycus'
SF activities 24 April to 22 May 2026: seven unanswered rep emails / event invites to Chong Ning Pang

decision_criteria.narrative: Chee Koon Tan laid out a concrete, criteria-led evaluation on 25 March 2026: NUS will run a hands-on market study using its own 'track leaders' (functional experts in sourcing, buying, supplier management, and catalog), each scoring the product against their real pain points in live demos, not slideware ('if I can't see and you just show me a bunch of slides, it's not very comforting... they will give us a score for each of the product'). Named must-have criteria are framework-agreement spend tracking, simple one-time supplier registration (including suppliers who refuse to register, e.g. utilities, Apple, charities), low-code/zero-code configuration ('we want a platform that mostly is configuration... there shouldn't be any code written'), AI maturity, full configuration, and a strong Supplier Portal. These are confirmed from the buyer's own words; the open gap is the actual expert scoring, which has not been observed.
sources: Avoma 25 March 2026, Chee Koon Tan (expert-scored market study, framework agreement, supplier registration, low-code, AI maturity)
SF Next_Step__c 25 March 2026 ('AI maturity, full configuration, Supplier Portal are of strong interest')

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is actually running is a two-phase play read directly from the 25 March 2026 call: first an internal, expert-led market study to get comfortable with the real products ('so that we know very in depth who can make it before we invite even the vendors for the tender'), then an open public tender the buyer intends to launch by the end of 2026, with a decision into early 2027 and a one-year Ariba extension bridging go-live. Chee Koon Tan runs the study and marshals the four functional experts; Chong Ning Pang (CPO) is the sponsor above him; and NUS's IT department carries real influence and currently leans to SAP ('very insistent that we give SAP a chance'). The approval chain beyond CPO and IT, and the public-sector procurement gates the tender will impose, are not yet documented. The near-term risk is that the market-study phase has stalled during Chee Koon Tan's medical leave, so Zycus is not yet locked into the pre-tender shortlist.
sources: Avoma 25 March 2026, Chee Koon Tan (expert market study then open tender, end-2026 timing, one-year Ariba extension)
SF Next_Step__c 25 March 2026 (tender launch end of Q3/Q4, influence RFP)

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have surfaced, which is correct for a Qualified, pre-tender education deal in Singapore: this is a new-business Source-to-Contract pursuit that has not reached commercials, so there is no MSA, Order Form, or SOW discussion yet, and none should be expected pre-RFP. The one forcing function on record is the SAP Ariba contract, which Chee Koon Tan said expires 'mid of next year' with a likely one-year extension, setting the real go-live clock. As a public university, NUS will run its selection through an open tender, so the eventual paper path will be tender-driven public procurement rather than a Zycus-paper-first motion. Visibility will come only once the tender documents publish; the move now is to influence the RFP requirements, not to chase paper.
sources: Avoma 25 March 2026, Chee Koon Tan ('our current contract with SAP is gonna expire by next year', one-year extension likely)
SF Stage Qualified / Next_Step__c (pre-tender, influence-RFP objective)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed, acute, and owned by the procurement function led by CPO Chong Ning Pang and run day-to-day by Chee Koon Tan. On 25 March 2026 Chee Koon Tan detailed three concrete failures of SAP Ariba: (1) no framework-agreement support, forcing manual reallocation of award value across suppliers with real over-commitment risk ('I actually exceeded what was approved... this is a big problem for us'); (2) a burdensome two-step supplier registration with no clean path for suppliers who refuse to register (utility providers, Apple, charities), which NUS today handles with fake accounts and outsourced teams; and (3) manual specification review and recommendation-report generation that drags sourcing cycles into months. SAP's promised NextGen fix is vaporware ('we asked to see the demo and they could not show us... their product is probably still on paper'). The pain is qualitative and vivid but not yet costed, which is the one thing holding the business case back.
sources: Avoma 25 March 2026, Chee Koon Tan (framework agreement, supplier registration, spec/recommendation pain, NextGen not demoable)
SF AIS_Why__c 25 March 2026 ('Ariba is too manual and not able to fix bug with new version (next Gen)')

champion.narrative: There is no confirmed champion yet, but Chee Koon Tan (Associate Director, Decision Maker) is a genuine developing one and the single best bet. He re-opened the relationship after Zycus's contact was lost ('when your email came, it was just in right just in'), led a rich 90-minute discovery himself, exposed NUS's real pain and its competitive shortlist unprompted, brought his organisation's evaluation approach into the open, and committed to marshal four functional experts for scored demos. What is still missing is the advocacy that makes a champion: he has not yet introduced Zycus to his boss CPO Chong Ning Pang, has not pushed back on IT's SAP bias for us, has shared no evaluation scoring, and has gone silent since 31 March while recovering from surgery. So he is engaged and well-disposed but unproven and currently cooling; developing him (a direct working relationship, the expert sessions, and a Pang introduction) is the top job before the tender.
sources: Avoma 25 March 2026, Chee Koon Tan (drove discovery, shared pain and shortlist, committed the four expert sessions)
SF OpportunityContactRole: Chee Koon Tan, Associate Director, Decision Maker
SF activities 12-15 May 2026 (rep notes referencing Chee Koon Tan's post-surgery recovery; no buyer reply since 31 March)

competition.narrative: NUS is running an explicit four-way market review, all named by Chee Koon Tan on 25 March 2026: incumbent SAP Ariba plus iValue, GEP, and Coupa ('we are exploring iValue, we are exploring GEP, we are also exploring Coupa'). SAP Ariba is the strongest current threat despite its weakness on the merits: it is the entrenched incumbent, its NextGen release could not be demoed ('their product is probably still on paper'), yet NUS's IT department is 'very insistent that we give SAP a chance', giving it political air cover, and NUS will extend the Ariba contract a year regardless. iValue, GEP, and Coupa are named alternatives with no sentiment captured yet, so their threat is live but unmeasured. Zycus's wedge is AI and native integration: the buyer volunteered that AI is 'very relevant' for spec generation and recommendation reports, and Zycus differentiated Merlin as natively built ('everything in Zycus has been natively built... that's a big differentiator versus almost any other vendor') against Coupa/SAP's acquire-and-bolt-on approach. The way to win is to convert that AI interest into influencing the tender requirements before SAP's IT sponsors shape them.
sources: Avoma 25 March 2026, Chee Koon Tan ('we are exploring iValue... GEP... also exploring Coupa'; 'IT department is very insistent that we give SAP a chance')
Avoma 25 March 2026, Guillaume Pasquet (native Merlin AI vs bolt-on competitors)

deal_movement.summary: Momentum has thinned since the strong 25 March 2026 discovery call. The buyer replied by email through 31 March (spec/registration questions), then went quiet during Chee Koon Tan's surgery recovery; the only movement since is Zycus rep outbound and a 1 July 2026 owner reassignment to Carl Kimball. No expert evaluation session or scoring has been observed.

items.change: Introduction / discovery call held; buyer named full competitive shortlist and pain (SAP Ariba, iValue, GEP, Coupa)
Stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified
Chee Koon Tan replied by email with spec-quality and supplier-registration questions (last genuine two-way buyer touch)
Zycus ran a rep outreach campaign (Procurement AI World Tour and Horizon 2026 SEA invites) to Chong Ning Pang and Chee Koon Tan; no buyer reply
Opportunity ownership reassigned to Carl Kimball (from Guillaume Pasquet)

competitive_position.summary: A four-way pre-tender market review named on 25 March 2026. SAP Ariba is the single strongest CURRENT threat: incumbent with a one-year extension and internal IT sponsorship ('very insistent we give SAP a chance'), even though its NextGen could not be demoed. GEP, iValue, and Coupa are live alternatives the buyer is actively exploring with no sentiment captured yet. Zycus's wedge is native Merlin AI and framework-agreement/registration fitment; the fight is won or lost by influencing the RFP requirements before IT frames them around SAP.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Ariba is still on the table, but it don't look good on them because... we asked to see the demo and they could not show us the demo still; our IT department is very insistent that we give SAP a chance.
competitors.how_we_win: Weaponise the framework-agreement and one-time supplier-registration gaps SAP cannot fix, and neutralise IT's bias by getting a working Merlin demo scored higher by NUS's own experts.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: We are exploring iValue. We are exploring GEP. We are also exploring Coupa.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead on native single-codebase integration and local Singapore data-centre/references (Fair Price, ST Engineering) before GEP anchors on price.

competitors.name: iValue
competitors.how_we_win: Show Merlin agentic maturity and configuration-over-code so the AI story separates Zycus from a feature-parity comparison.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Zycus's natively built intake/Merlin against Coupa's acquire-and-integrate approach ('vendors acquiring different pieces of modules... it creates strong dependency').

customer_expectations_fit.summary: NUS wants a configuration-first, AI-mature S2C platform that fixes SAP Ariba's framework-agreement and supplier-registration failures and can be proven in live, expert-scored demos. Zycus is well aligned on AI and native integration; the exposed edges are the unregistered-supplier workflow and the unproven expert-scoring outcome.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage, pre-tender influence deal that has cooled during the champion's medical leave. The win levers are re-engagement, multi-threading to power, and locking Zycus into the RFP requirements before SAP's IT sponsors do.
flags: Re-engage Chee Koon Tan now and restart the four expert-scored demo sessions; the market study that shapes NUS's tender has stalled since his surgery and that is where Zycus wins or loses the shortlist.
Multi-thread beyond a single Decision Maker to CPO Chong Ning Pang, the named economic buyer who has never been on a call, before the tender scope is written.

Influence the RFP requirements around framework-agreement tracking, one-time supplier registration, and native Merlin AI, the exact gaps SAP Ariba cannot demo, to counter IT's 'give SAP a chance' bias.

champion_strength.summary: Chee Koon Tan is a developing champion, not yet a confirmed one. He re-opened the relationship, drove a strong discovery, exposed NUS's pain and full competitive shortlist, and committed the expert sessions, but has not introduced Zycus to CPO Chong Ning Pang, has not advocated against IT's SAP bias, has shared no scoring, and has been silent since 31 March while recovering from surgery. Real but unproven and currently cooling.
champion_strength.source: Avoma 25 March 2026; SF OpportunityContactRole; SF activities through 22 May 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus landed a native-AI narrative on 25 March: Merlin agentic platform built organically vs competitors' acquire-and-bolt-on approach, plus conversational intake on Teams and a 100+ agent library. The buyer volunteered that AI is 'very relevant' for spec generation and recommendation reports, so the positioning is resonating but has not yet been proven in a scored, use-case demo.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6 (Moderate) is fair: the buyer originated the AI interest ('AI is very relevant for us') and named concrete AI use cases (spec generation, recommendation reports), but only Merlin Intake is in scope, the pain is not yet AI-quantified, and the tender is months out. The calls corroborate, not contradict, the field.

items.detail: NUS IT department is pushing to give incumbent SAP Ariba a chance despite its failed NextGen demo, providing political cover to the incumbent.
Economic buyer CPO Chong Ning Pang has never engaged directly; the deal is single-threaded through a Decision Maker who is on medical leave.
items.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; SF activities through 22 May 2026

items.detail: Buyer's tender is not until end of 2026 with a one-year Ariba extension, and engagement has cooled to zero two-way contact since 31 March, risking loss of pre-tender position.
Scope is framed as phased Source-to-Contract first with Pay/P2P deferred to a later tender, a defensive, cost-controlled posture rather than a full-suite commitment.

No education-sector reference has been put in front of NUS yet; Zycus cited Fair Price, ST Engineering, MUFG, and Catholic Education Australia on the intro call but none tied to a public-university procurement proof.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has cooled: the last genuine two-way buyer exchange was 31 March 2026, with only unanswered Zycus outbound since. The silence is partly explained by Chee Koon Tan's surgery recovery, so this is a stall to restart rather than a loss, but the deal is not currently progressing.
items: Zero buyer reply on any thread since 31 March 2026 despite seven rep touches through 22 May.
Rep confidence logged at 10% on 27 April 2026.
No expert evaluation session or scoring confirmed as held.

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay (the 2020-era review and NUS's broader procurement transformation)
scope_change.to: Source-to-Contract first (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, Merlin Intake), with Pay/P2P deferred to a later tender phase
scope_change.detail: Chee Koon Tan framed a phased approach on 25 March 2026: cover current needs first and 'future one, that one we can still put it on a tender when we want people to implement.' The deal name NUS_S2C and the S2C module set reflect a narrowed, phased, cost-controlled entry rather than a full-suite commitment.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early, pre-tender influence play at NUS that has gone quiet: Chee Koon Tan drove a strong discovery and named a four-way shortlist, but he is on medical leave, the CPO has never engaged, incumbent SAP has IT air cover, and Zycus has to re-earn a shortlist slot before the end-2026 tender.

win_position.text: This sits low because NUS is running an even four-way market review (SAP Ariba, iValue, GEP, Coupa) with no Zycus lean yet and the buyer only 'exploring' each per the 25 March 2026 call.

Zycus is genuinely in the game: Chee Koon Tan re-opened the relationship himself and volunteered that AI is 'very relevant' and Ariba is 'too manual' with a NextGen it cannot even demo (25 March 2026).
Incumbent SAP Ariba has internal air cover, 'our IT department is very insistent that we give SAP a chance', so the political lean is toward the incumbent, not us (Chee Koon Tan, 25 March 2026).
The only named economic buyer, CPO Chong Ning Pang, has never been on a call and has not replied to seven outreach touches, so there is no confirmed access to power.

deal_momentum.text: No genuine two-way buyer contact since Chee Koon Tan's 31 March 2026 email; everything since is unanswered Zycus outbound through 22 May 2026.
The expert-scored demo sessions Chee Koon Tan committed on 25 March 2026 have not been confirmed as held, so the evaluation that decides the tender shortlist has stalled.
The stall has a benign cause: rep notes on 12 and 15 May 2026 reference Chee Koon Tan recovering from surgery, so this is a restart, not a loss.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer proposed a serious, structured commitment on 25 March 2026: four to five functional experts scoring each module hands-on, plus a stated intent to launch a tender by end of 2026.
That commitment is not yet actioned: NUS still owes the expert names and dates, and no session, scoring, or CPO sponsorship has materialised.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded through one Decision Maker who is on medical leave, with the CPO economic buyer never engaged, is the core early-stage risk.
Scope has narrowed to Source-to-Contract with Pay deferred, a defensive phased posture that signals budget caution.
Incumbent SAP Ariba with a one-year extension and IT political backing can shape the tender requirements before Zycus is locked in.

champion_strength.evidence: Developing but unproven and cooling: Chee Koon Tan drove discovery but has not introduced the CPO, advocated internally, or made contact since 31 March 2026.

customer_preference.evidence: Even four-way exploration with no Zycus lean and IT favouring incumbent SAP (25 March 2026).

momentum.evidence: Zero buyer reply since 31 March 2026; expert evaluation sessions never confirmed as held.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer is exploring an even field ('we are exploring iValue... GEP... also exploring Coupa') with no Zycus-specific lean, and IT wants to 'give SAP a chance' (Chee Koon Tan, 25 March 2026).

business_case.evidence: Vivid qualitative pain (framework-agreement failures, manual registration consuming outsourced spend, months-long spec cycles) but no dollar, headcount, or cycle-time figure quantified.

No quantified business case; pain is qualitative only.
